result:
I'd eliminate all forms of artificial scarcity—whether it's food, energy, housing, or information—by accelerating the deployment of proven, abundant technologies like nuclear fusion, vertical farming, desalination, and open-source AI-driven automation. 

Why? Human suffering stems largely from outdated systems enforcing scarcity (e.g., fossil fuel dependencies, intellectual property hoarding, inefficient agriculture) despite our tech already solving these at scale. Fusion alone could provide limitless clean energy (ITER and private ventures like Commonwealth Fusion are on track for net-positive by 2030s), slashing costs for everything else. Data: Global hunger persists for 783 million people (UN 2023) amid food waste equaling 1.3 billion tons/year, fixable with precision ag and distribution bots. No more wars over resources, no poverty traps, just exponential progress. The world becomes a post-scarcity playground for creativity and exploration.
